meanings.definition: A greeting used in the morning, equivalent to 'good morning'.
A formal greeting used during daytime, equivalent to 'good day' or 'good afternoon'.
meanings.example: Laba diena, ar galiu jums padėti?

meanings.definition: A greeting used in the evening, equivalent to 'good evening'.
An informal greeting equivalent to 'hello' or 'hi', used in casual settings.
A farewell or greeting used at night or before going to sleep, equivalent to 'good night'.
A formal farewell expression equivalent to 'goodbye'.
An expression of gratitude, equivalent to 'thank you' or 'thanks'.
meanings.example: Ačiū, kad man padėjai su namų darbais.

meanings.definition: Used as a polite response to thanks, equivalent to 'you're welcome', or as an invitation meaning 'please, go ahead'.
An expression used to apologize or to politely get someone's attention, equivalent to 'excuse me' or 'I'm sorry'.
meanings.example: Atsiprašau, ar galėtumėte man pasakyti, kiek valandų?

meanings.definition: A response to thanks meaning 'you're welcome' or 'don't mention it', literally 'there's nothing to thank for'.
meanings.example: — Ačiū už pagalbą! — Nėra už ką!

meanings.definition: A polite expression used when meeting someone, equivalent to 'nice to meet you' or 'pleased to meet you'.
meanings.example: Labai malonu, mano vardas yra Jonas.

meanings.definition: An exclamation expressing approval or satisfaction, meaning 'great', 'excellent', or 'wonderful'.
An adverb meaning 'well' or 'good', also used as an affirmative response meaning 'okay' or 'all right'.
meanings.example: Gerai, aš sutinku su tavo pasiūlymu.

meanings.definition: An affirmative particle meaning 'yes', used to express agreement or confirmation.
meanings.example: — Ar tu nori kavos? — Taip, prašau.

meanings.definition: A negative particle meaning 'no', used to express disagreement, denial, or refusal.
At the present time or moment.
In addition; likewise.
meanings.example: Aš taip pat noriu eiti į kiną.

meanings.definition: No more than; merely; exclusively.
To a small extent or degree.
A small amount or to a small degree.
A word or set of words by which a person is known; a first name.
A hereditary family name passed down from one generation to the next.
To exist or live; used as a copula linking a subject with a predicate.

Second person singular present tense form of 'būti' (to be).

Third person past negative form of 'būti' (to be); indicates absence or non-existence in the past.

Third person past tense form of 'dirbti' (to work).

Past tense of 'kalbėti'; spoke or talked
To engage in learning, especially at a university

To comprehend the meaning of something

Past tense of 'suprasti'; understood

A female dancer who performs dance professionally or as a hobby.

meanings.definition: The spring season, when weather gets warmer and plants begin to grow.
meanings.example: Pavasaris atneša daugiau saulės ir žydinčių gėlių.

meanings.definition: The summer season, usually the warmest part of the year.
The autumn season, when leaves change color and temperatures drop.

meanings.definition: The winter season, usually the coldest part of the year.

meanings.definition: A period of seven consecutive days.
meanings.example: Po savaitės vėl susitiksime tame pačiame biure.

meanings.definition: Monday, the first workday of the week in many calendars.
Tuesday, the day following Monday.
Wednesday, the day between Tuesday and Thursday.
meanings.example: Trečiadienis dažnai būna pati darbingiausia savaitės diena.

meanings.definition: Thursday, the day between Wednesday and Friday.
meanings.example: Ketvirtadienį vakare einu į sporto salę.

meanings.definition: Friday, the day before the weekend.
meanings.example: Penktadienį po darbo susitinkame su draugais.

meanings.definition: Saturday, typically the first day of the weekend.
meanings.example: Šeštadienis skirtas poilsiui ir namų tvarkymui.

meanings.definition: Sunday, typically the second day of the weekend.
The early part of the day, from dawn until around noon.
The part of a twenty-four-hour period when it is light, or a single calendar day.
meanings.example: Ši diena buvo ilga, bet labai produktyvi.

meanings.definition: The part of the day between late afternoon and night.
The period of darkness between evening and morning.
At all times; on every occasion.
At no time; not ever.
meanings.example: Aš niekada nevėluoju į svarbius susitikimus.

meanings.definition: On many occasions; frequently.
meanings.example: Mes dažnai einame pasivaikščioti po darbo.

meanings.definition: From time to time; occasionally.
On this day; at the present day.
On the day after today.
On each day; daily.
The period of Saturday and Sunday, typically free from work.

qaNote: "savaitė" removed from antonymTerms: savaitgalis is a component of savaitė (the weekend is part of the week), not its opposite. Antonym corrected to "darbo dienos" only.

meanings.definition: The number 0; nothing.

qaNote: Term capitalization corrected from "Nulis" to "nulis"; Lithuanian common nouns are written in lowercase.

meanings.definition: The number 1; a single unit.

qaNote: "pirmas" removed from synonyms: "pirmas" is the ordinal numeral (first), not a synonym of the cardinal "vienas" (one). Moved to relatedTerms.

meanings.definition: The number 2; one more than one.
The number 3; one more than two.
The number 4; one more than three.
The number 5; one more than four.
meanings.example: Aš dirbu penkias dienas per savaitę.

meanings.definition: The number 6; one more than five.
The number 7; one more than six.
The number 8; one more than seven.
The number 9; one more than eight.
The number 10; one more than nine.

qaNote: "dešimtas" removed from synonyms: "dešimtas" is the ordinal numeral (tenth), not a synonym of the cardinal "dešimt" (ten). Moved to relatedTerms.

meanings.definition: A married woman in relation to her husband.

qaNote: "nepažįstamoji" (female stranger) removed from antonymTerms: a stranger is not a meaningful antonym of wife. Antonym corrected to "vyras" only.

meanings.definition: A male sibling.

qaNote: "nepažįstamasis" (male stranger) removed from antonymTerms: a stranger is not a meaningful antonym of brother. Antonym corrected to "sesuo" only.

meanings.definition: A female sibling.

qaNote: "nepažįstamoji" (female stranger) removed from antonymTerms: a stranger is not a meaningful antonym of sister. Antonym corrected to "brolis" only.

meanings.definition: A daughter of one's uncle or aunt.

qaNote: "nepažįstamoji" (female stranger) removed from antonymTerms: a stranger is not a meaningful antonym of female cousin. Replaced with "pusbrolis" (male cousin) as the gender-contrasting counterpart.

meanings.definition: A young human being below the age of puberty.

qaNote: "kūdikis" removed from synonyms: kūdikis (infant/baby) covers only the earliest stage of childhood, whereas vaikas covers children in general; kūdikis is a hyponym, not a synonym. Moved to relatedTerms.

meanings.definition: A very young child or infant.
A son of one's son or daughter.
A daughter of one's son or daughter.
A woman who has given birth to a child.
An informal word for mother.
A man who has begotten a child.
An informal word for father.
A person's father and mother.

qaNote: "nepažįstamieji" (strangers) removed from antonymTerms: strangers are not a meaningful antonym of parents. Antonym corrected to "vaikai" only.

meanings.definition: A male child in relation to his parents.

qaNote: "berniukas" (boy) removed from synonyms: berniukas denotes any male child, while sūnus specifically denotes a male child in relation to his parents; the relational sense is absent from berniukas. Moved to relatedTerms.

meanings.definition: A female child in relation to her parents.

qaNote: "mergaitė" (girl) removed from synonyms: mergaitė denotes any female child, while duktė specifically denotes a female child in relation to her parents; the relational sense is absent from mergaitė. Moved to relatedTerms.

meanings.definition: The father of one's parent.
The mother of one's parent.

qaNote: "senoji moteris" (old woman) removed from synonyms: an old woman is not synonymous with a grandmother; advanced age does not imply the grandparent relationship. Remaining synonym "tėvo motina" is retained. | "tėvo motina" (father's mother) is the sole synonym but specifies only the paternal grandmother; "senelė" refers to either paternal or maternal grandmother. The synonym should be generalised to "mamos/tėvo mama" or replaced with a form covering both lines (e.g., "prosenelė" is wrong; correct option: "močiutė").

meanings.definition: Affectionate diminutive form of 'senelė', used to address or refer to one's grandmother.
The father of one's grandfather or grandmother; a male ancestor two generations above the grandparents.
meanings.example: Mano prosenelis gyveno kaime ir dirbo žemę.

qaNote: antonymTerms uses cross-gender generational pairing ["proanūkė"]; corrected to ["prosenelė"] to match gender-pair pattern used throughout (tėvas↔motina, dėdė↔teta, pusbrolis↔pusseserė)

meanings.definition: The mother of one's grandfather or grandmother; a female ancestor two generations above the grandparents.

qaNote: antonymTerms uses cross-gender generational pairing ["proanūkis"]; corrected to ["prosenelis"] to match gender-pair pattern used throughout

meanings.definition: The brother of one's mother or father, or the husband of one's aunt.
meanings.example: Dėdė Jonas atvyko pas mus per Kalėdas.

meanings.definition: The sister of one's mother or father, or the wife of one's uncle.
A male child of one's uncle or aunt; a first cousin of the male gender.
meanings.example: Mano pusbrolis gyvena kitame mieste, bet mes dažnai susitinkame.

meanings.definition: Describes a man who is legally bound in marriage to a woman.
Describes a woman who is legally bound in marriage to a man.
meanings.example: Ji yra ištekėjusi ir turi du vaikus.

meanings.definition: Describes a man whose marriage has been legally dissolved.
meanings.example: Jis išsiskyręs ir augina vaikus vienas.

meanings.definition: Describes a woman whose marriage has been legally dissolved.
Describes a male person who has died; no longer living.
Describes a female person who has died; no longer living.
meanings.example: Jo motina mirusi jau keletą metų.

meanings.definition: To have the ability, permission, or possibility to do something.
To feel deep affection, attachment, or romantic love for someone or something.
To have a desire or wish for something; to feel inclined to do something.
To feel longing or sadness because of the absence of a person, place, or thing.
meanings.example: Ji pasiilgo namų, kai išvyko studijuoti į užsienį.

qaNote: relatedTerms contained "atsiilgimas" (means rest/recovery from fatigue) which is semantically unrelated to longing; removed from relatedTerms

meanings.definition: To be in a seated position; to rest with the weight of the body on the buttocks.
meanings.example: Vaikai sėdi prie stalo ir piešia.

qaNote: "tupėti" removed from synonyms: tupėti means to squat or crouch in a low, bent-knee position, which is a distinct posture from sitting (sėdėti). Entry now has 1 synonym ("ilsėtis sėdint"); a second synonym is needed.

meanings.definition: To be in an upright position on one's feet without moving.
meanings.example: Jis stovi prie lango ir žiūri į lauką.

meanings.definition: To own or possess something; to hold something as a property or attribute.
meanings.example: Aš turiu du brolius ir vieną seserį.

meanings.definition: To direct one's gaze at something; to watch attentively.
To perceive sounds through the sense of hearing.
To find something pleasing or enjoyable; to have a preference for something.
meanings.example: Ji mėgsta skaityti knygas prieš miegą.

qaNote: "mylėti" removed from synonyms: mylėti means to love (strong emotional attachment), which is semantically stronger than mėgti (to like/enjoy); they are not synonymous. Entry now has 1 synonym ("pamėgti"); a second synonym is needed.

meanings.definition: To meet someone for the first time and introduce oneself; to become familiar with someone or something.
meanings.example: Mes susipažinome universitete pirmojo kurso pradžioje.

translation: to get acquainted, to meet (for the first time)
qaNote: "pažinti" removed from synonyms: pažinti means to know or recognise someone already familiar, whereas susipažinti means to meet and become acquainted for the first time; these are opposite points on the acquaintance timeline. Entry now has 1 synonym ("prisistatyti"); a second synonym is needed.

meanings.definition: To engage in play or a game for fun; to take part in a sport or recreational activity.

qaNote: "pramogauti" removed from synonyms: pramogauti means to have fun or entertain oneself (a broader leisure concept), while žaisti specifically means to play a game or sport; the two overlap but are not synonymous. Entry now has 0 synonyms; at least 1–2 synonyms needed (e.g., "žaidžioti", "sportuoti").

meanings.definition: Having lived or existed for only a short time; not old. Masculine form.

qaNote: Term capitalisation corrected from "Jaunas" to "jaunas"; Lithuanian adjectives and common nouns are written in lowercase unless beginning a sentence.

meanings.definition: Having lived or existed for only a short time; not old. Feminine form.
meanings.example: Ji yra jauna ir pilna energijos.

qaNote: "jaunoji" removed from synonyms: jaunoji is the definite (specific) feminine form used in ceremonial or literary contexts (notably meaning "the bride"); it does not function as a plain synonym of the attributive adjective jauna (young, fem.). Entry now has 1 synonym ("jaunatvišką"); a second synonym is needed.

meanings.definition: Having lived or existed for a long time; advanced in age. Masculine form.
meanings.example: Senas žmogus išmintingas, nes daug matęs.

meanings.definition: Having lived or existed for a long time; advanced in age. Feminine form.
meanings.example: Sena moteris sėdėjo suole ir mezgė.

meanings.definition: Feeling or showing happiness and high spirits; merry. Masculine form.
Feeling or showing happiness and high spirits; merry. Feminine form.
Experiencing or expressing sorrow or unhappiness.
meanings.example: Jis atrodė labai liūdnas po nesėkmės.

meanings.definition: Experiencing or expressing sorrow; feminine/neuter form of liūdnas.
meanings.example: Mergaitė atrodė labai liūdna po įvykio.

meanings.definition: Having qualities that give pleasure to the senses; attractive in appearance.
meanings.example: Tai labai gražus miestas su sena architektūra.

meanings.definition: Having an attractive appearance; feminine form of gražus.
meanings.example: Ji yra labai graži moteris su malonios šypsena.

meanings.definition: Lacking beauty; unpleasant to look at.
meanings.example: Jis manė, kad pastatas yra negražus ir modernūs.

meanings.definition: Lacking beauty; unpleasant to look at; feminine form of negražus.
meanings.example: Ji laikė save negraži, nors kiti taip visai nemanė.

meanings.definition: Emitting or reflecting a lot of light; full of light.
meanings.example: Šviesus kambarys pakelia nuotaiką žiemos rytais.

meanings.definition: Emitting or reflecting much light; feminine form of šviesus.
meanings.example: Šviesi palata padėjo pacientui atsigauti greičiau.

meanings.definition: Having little or no light; of a deep or shadowy colour.
meanings.example: Tamsus koridorius atrodė paslaptingai ir keistai.

meanings.definition: Having little or no light; feminine form of tamsus.
meanings.example: Tamsi naktis padengė miestą tyliu šydu.

meanings.definition: Not high in physical position or elevation; below average height.
meanings.example: Žemas medis augo šalia kelio ir teikė šešėlį.

meanings.definition: Not high in position or elevation; feminine/neuter form of žemas.
meanings.example: Žema tvora neapsaugojo sodo nuo vėjo.

meanings.definition: Of great height; extending far upward.
meanings.example: Aukštas bokštas matėsi iš labai toli.

meanings.definition: Of great height; feminine/neuter form of aukštas.
meanings.example: Aukšta moteris išsiskyrė minioje savo išvaizda.

meanings.definition: Of considerable size, bulk or weight; stocky or heavily built.
meanings.example: Stambus vyras lengvai pakėlė sunkią dėžę.

meanings.definition: Of considerable size or bulk; feminine form of stambus.
meanings.example: Stambi moteris stovėjo prie įėjimo durų.

meanings.definition: Of small thickness or width; slim or slender in build.
meanings.example: Plonas berniukas greitai bėgo tarp medžių.

meanings.definition: Of small thickness; slim in build; feminine form of plonas.
meanings.example: Plona mergina vilkėjo dideliais ir platiais drabužiais.

meanings.definition: Giving a sense of happy satisfaction or enjoyment; agreeable or pleasing.
meanings.example: Malonus žmogus lengvai susiranda naujų draugų.

meanings.definition: Giving a sense of happy satisfaction; feminine form of malonus.
meanings.example: Maloni šeimininkė pakvietė mus prie bendro stalo.

meanings.definition: Unpleasant to look at or experience; repulsive or disgusting.
meanings.example: Bjaurus oras privertė mus likti namie visą dieną.

meanings.definition: Unpleasant in appearance or character; feminine form of bjaurus.
meanings.example: Bjauri šypsena išdavė jo tikruosius ketinimus.

meanings.definition: Feeling or showing pleasure or contentment; fortunate and in a good mood.
meanings.example: Laimingas vaikas šoko per visą kambarį linksmai.

meanings.definition: Feeling or showing contentment or joy; feminine form of laimingas.
meanings.example: Laiminga mama apkabino savo mažą vaiką.

meanings.definition: Not feeling or showing happiness; unfortunate or miserable.
meanings.example: Nelaimingas berniukas sėdėjo vienas kampe tylos.

meanings.definition: Not feeling or showing happiness; feminine form of nelaimingas.
meanings.example: Nelaiminga moteris verkė viena tuščiame kambaryje.

meanings.definition: Endearing, sweet or lovable in character or appearance.
meanings.example: Mielas šuniukas sekė paskui ją visur neatstumdamas.

meanings.definition: Endearing or sweet in nature; feminine form of mielas.
meanings.example: Miela mergaitė padovanojo gėlių savo mokytojai.

meanings.definition: Feeling or displaying anger; fierce or hostile in disposition.
meanings.example: Piktas šuo lojo ant visų pravažiuojančių mašinų.

meanings.definition: Feeling or displaying anger; feminine form of piktas.
meanings.example: Pikta katė išsipūtė ir garsiai šnypštė.

meanings.definition: Recently made, acquired, or come into existence; not existing before.
meanings.example: Jis pirko naują automobilį praėjusią savaitę.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'naujas': recently made or come into existence.
meanings.example: Ji įsikėlė į naują butą miesto centre.

meanings.definition: Unusual or unexpected; provoking surprise or curiosity.
meanings.example: Tas keistas garsas naktį neleido man užmigti.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'keistas': unusual or unexpected.
meanings.example: Man atrodo keista, kad jis neatėjo į susitikimą.

meanings.definition: Arousing curiosity or attention; engaging the mind.
meanings.example: Ši knyga labai įdomi – negaliu jos padėti.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'įdomus': arousing curiosity or attention.
meanings.example: Tai labai įdomi idėja – verta apsvarstyti.

meanings.definition: Not interesting; failing to hold attention; tedious.
meanings.example: Paskaita buvo tokia nuobodi, kad pusė studentų užmigo.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'nuobodus': not interesting; tedious.
meanings.example: Ši nuobodi rutina mane slegia jau kelis mėnesius.

meanings.definition: Showing kindness and goodwill; inclined to make friends.
meanings.example: Mūsų kaimynas labai draugiškas – visada pasisveikina.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'draugiškas': showing kindness and goodwill.
meanings.example: Ji tokia draugiška – greitai susidraugauja su visais.

meanings.definition: Having or showing good reasoning ability; clever and sensible.
meanings.example: Protingas vaikas greitai supranta naują medžiagą.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'protingas': having good reasoning ability.
meanings.example: Ji protinga moteris ir visada randa išeitį iš sudėtingų situacijų.

meanings.definition: Having great physical power or force; robust and powerful.
meanings.example: Stiprus vyras lengvai pakėlė sunkų dėžę.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'stiprus': having great physical or inner power.
meanings.example: Ji stipri moteris ir atlaikė visus sunkumus.

meanings.definition: Lacking physical strength or power; not strong or robust.
meanings.example: Po ligos jis jautėsi silpnas ir pavargęs.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'silpnas': lacking physical or emotional strength.
meanings.example: Senolė buvo silpna ir sunkiai vaikščiojo.

meanings.definition: Making very little noise; calm and silent.
meanings.example: Naktį miestas buvo tylus ir ramus.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'tylus': making very little noise; calm.
meanings.example: Ji tyli mergaitė, bet labai drąsi.

meanings.definition: Making or full of a lot of noise; loud and clamorous.
meanings.example: Triukšmingas žaidimų aikštelė buvo pilna vaikų.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'triukšmingas': making or full of a lot of noise.
meanings.example: Klasė buvo labai triukšminga – vaikai nesustodami kalbėjo.

meanings.definition: Moving or acting with little speed; not fast.
meanings.example: Lėtas vėžlys negali pabėgti nuo plėšrūnų.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'lėtas': moving or acting with little speed.
Moving or capable of moving at high speed; swift.
meanings.example: Greitas automobilis lenkė kitus per varžybas.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'greitas': moving at high speed; swift.
meanings.example: Greita reakcija išgelbėjo jį nuo nelaimingo atsitikimo.

meanings.definition: Extremely good; outstanding in quality or performance.
meanings.example: Studentas gavo puikų įvertinimą už savo darbą.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'puikus': extremely good; outstanding.
meanings.example: Tai puiki proga parodyti savo gebėjimus.

meanings.definition: Having desirable or positive qualities; satisfactory or pleasing.
meanings.example: Jis visada buvo geras draugas ir pagelbėdavo sunkią akimirką.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'geras': having desirable or positive qualities.
meanings.example: Gera knyga gali pakeisti tavo požiūrį į pasaulį.

meanings.definition: Having negative qualities; not good; harmful or unpleasant.
meanings.example: Blogas oras sutrukdė mūsų lauko renginį.

meanings.definition: Feminine form of 'blogas': having negative qualities; harmful or unpleasant.
meanings.example: Bloga nuotaika praėjo po ilgo pasivaikščiojimo.

meanings.definition: Of great size or extent; large in physical dimensions (masculine singular nominative form).
meanings.example: Tas didelis namas stovi kalno viršuje.

To sample or taste food or drink, usually in small amounts.
To consume food by chewing and swallowing.
To consume a liquid by swallowing.
To eat the first meal of the day, typically in the morning.
To eat the midday meal, typically around noon.
To eat the evening meal, usually the last main meal of the day.

translation: to have dinner / to have supper

meanings.definition: To ask someone to come, attend an event, or participate in something.
meanings.example: Noriu pakviesti tave į savo gimtadienio vakarėlį.

meanings.definition: To remain in a place or state until a particular time or event occurs.
To cook food in boiling water or another liquid; also describes the process of liquid reaching boiling point.
To cook food using dry heat, in an oven, on a pan, or over a fire.

translation: to bake / to fry / to roast

meanings.definition: To perform an action, carry out a task, or create something.
To grasp, pick up, or receive something with the hands or figuratively.
To hand over, provide, or offer something to someone.
To cause a liquid or loose substance to flow from a container.
To put an object in a particular position or location.
To combine or stir ingredients or substances together.
To divide food or other material into pieces using a knife or similar tool.
To make food or a liquid slightly warmer, typically by reheating leftovers.
To give a particular impression or appearance; to seem to be a certain way.

translation: to seem / to look / to appear

meanings.definition: To hold an opinion, believe, or suppose something to be true.
meanings.example: Manau, kad šis receptas yra puikus.

translation: to think / to suppose / to believe

meanings.definition: To express a desire for something or ask someone to do something politely.
To form letters, words, or symbols on a surface; to compose text.
To look at and comprehend written or printed text.
The main meal eaten in the middle of the day, typically around noon.
meanings.example: Pietūs yra patiekiami nuo dvylikos iki antros.

meanings.definition: The first meal of the day, eaten in the morning.
meanings.example: Rytą valgome pusryčius su kiaušiniais ir duona.

meanings.definition: The evening meal, typically the last main meal of the day.
meanings.example: Vakarienė mūsų šeimoje paprastai būna septintą valandą.

meanings.definition: A thick, soured dairy product made from cream, widely used in Lithuanian cuisine.
The high-fat portion of milk that rises to the top; used in cooking and desserts.
A thick, creamy dairy product made by fermenting milk with bacteria.
meanings.example: Aš kasdien valgau jogurtą su uogomis.

meanings.definition: A fermented milk drink with a tangy flavor and probiotic properties.
Naturally soured milk; a traditional fermented dairy product in Lithuanian cuisine.
meanings.example: Rūgpienis yra geras priedas prie lietuviško juodos duonos.

meanings.definition: A sweet frozen dessert made from cream or milk, often flavoured.
A white liquid produced by mammals, used as a drink and in cooking.
A solid dairy food made from curdled milk, available in many varieties.
meanings.example: Ant sumuštinio uždėk šiek tiek sūrio.

meanings.definition: A staple food made by baking a dough of flour and water, often leavened with yeast.
An oval or round object laid by a female bird, especially a hen, used as food.
A pungent spice from dried peppercorns; also sweet or hot bell peppers used as a vegetable.
meanings.example: Pridėkite druskos ir pipirų pagal skonį.

meanings.definition: A white crystalline substance used to season or preserve food.
A viscous liquid derived from plants, used in cooking.
meanings.example: Kepant daržoves reikia šiek tiek aliejaus.

meanings.definition: A sweet crystalline substance obtained from sugar cane or beet, used to sweeten food and drinks.
A food made from flour and water shaped into various forms and boiled before eating.
meanings.example: Pietums virtome makaronus su pomidorų padažu.

meanings.definition: A cereal grain that is a staple food in many countries, cooked by boiling or steaming.
A clear, colourless liquid essential for all life forms; the most common drink.
A hot drink made by infusing dried tea leaves in boiling water.
Having a high temperature.
meanings.example: Arbata dar labai karšta — palauk šiek tiek.

meanings.definition: Containing nothing; not filled or occupied.
meanings.example: Šaldytuvas jau tuščias — reikia nueiti į parduotuvę.

meanings.definition: Of great weight; needing much effort to lift or move.
Requiring much effort or skill; not easy.
To have a specified price.
To exchange something for money; to give in return for payment.
To obtain something in exchange for money.
meanings.example: Šiandien einu į parduotuvę pirkti maisto.

meanings.definition: To come or go back to a place or person after being away.
meanings.example: Grįšiu namo po darbo apie septintą.

meanings.definition: To operate and control a motor vehicle.
meanings.example: Jis vairuoja automobilį jau dešimt metų.

meanings.definition: To cease moving or operating; to come to a halt.
To hold and support something while moving from one place to another.
meanings.example: Ar gali padėti nešti šiuos maišus?

meanings.definition: To give money in exchange for goods or services.
A hard surface built for vehicles to travel on; a route or path from one place to another.
meanings.example: Šis kelias veda tiesiai į miestą.

meanings.definition: One of the twelve periods into which a year is divided, lasting 28 to 31 days.
A period of twelve months, especially from 1 January to 31 December.
The indefinite continued progress of existence; a measured or measurable period.
Having a great extent from one side to the other.
meanings.example: Šis kelias labai platus — juo gali važiuoti du autobusai.

meanings.definition: Following something in time; subsequently.
meanings.example: Pirmiau pavalgykime, o po to eisime pasivaikščioti.

meanings.definition: A building designed for people to live in; one's place of residence.
meanings.example: Mes gyvename dideliame name prie parko.

meanings.definition: To move so as to uncover or allow access; to make open.
To move so as to cover an opening; to shut.
Free from dirt, marks, or stains.
Arranged neatly and in order; well organised.
To examine or inspect something to confirm its correctness or condition.

translation: to check, to verify, to examine

meanings.definition: To suffer from an illness or disease.
meanings.example: Šią savaitę sergu ir negaliu ateiti į darbą.

translation: to be ill, to be sick

meanings.definition: To make musical sounds with the voice, especially words set to a tune.
meanings.example: Vaikai dainuoja liaudies dainas per šventę.
